leads.name: AgriFarm Uganda Ltd
leads.profile: AgriFarm Uganda is an agricultural intelligence company operating in Uganda since 2007. It imports and distributes genuine agro-inputs, deploys field agronomists, and builds cooperative ecosystems to turn smallholder plots into reliable businesses across 120+ communities. The company offers six core services: Agronomy & Field Advisory (soil testing, IPM design, yield audits), Importation of Agro-Inputs (direct sourcing from accredited European, Asian, and African manufacturers), Distribution Network (2 Kampala depots, 14 regional dealers, last-mile village agents), Capacity Building (training-of-trainers programs for cooperatives, youth, women, and refugee-host populations; 18,400 trained), Commercial Nursery Beds (1.8M certified seedlings annually for coffee, fruit, agroforestry, and horticulture), and Immersive Field Schools (demo plots and farmer-to-farmer learning). Key programs include DRDIP (Development Response to Displacement Impacts Project, 2019-2025, 235K trees planted, 42 solar boreholes, 1.18M people reached), GOAL Uganda Resilient Livelihoods & Nutrition Programme (12,000 households, 640 nutrition gardens), UNHCR/OPM Refugee Settlement Agro-Inputs Programme (7 settlements, 210 cooperatives, 3,400 ha cultivated), CropLife Uganda Responsible Crop Protection Coalition (chaired by AgriFarm's MD, national stewardship policy, 1,200 dealers certified), FAO/MAAIF Fall Armyworm Response (4 districts, 9,800 farmers trained, -42% infestation), and Private agribusiness partnerships (11 exporter partners, +18% premium grade). The company is led by Managing Director Ms. Agnes Mbabazi, who also chairs CropLife Uganda. AgriFarm operates across 10 districts (Adjumani, Yumbe, Arua, Moyo, Isingiro, Kyegegwa, Kamwenge, Hoima, Kikuube, Kampala HQ) serving 5.60 million host population and 1,146,000 refugees. The company is a member of CropLife Uganda and describes itself as 'The Living Ecosystem of African Agriculture™' and 'Climate-Smart Agriculture Intelligence.' Contact: Head Office at Bugolobi Hardware City, Mulwana Road, Plot 6, RM103B; Outlet at Dream Plaza, Suite D72, Nakivubo Place; Phone: +256 (0) 755 888 850 / +256 (0) 757 771 330.

leads.name: Agro Supply
leads.profile:
# Agro Supply (Agro Supply Limited)

Agro Supply is a Farming company. Agro Supply is a company that builds the resilience of smallholder farmers with climate-smart agriculture using their innovative save-to-buy layaway model. They enable debt-free financing options for climate-resilient agri products and services. They work with stakeholders across the agricultural value chain to connect agri-inputs and financial services providers with their growing network of over 20. Agro Supply employs 7 people and has an annual revenue of $310.0K. Headquartered in Kampala, Uganda. Has $200.0K in total funding.

## About
We developed a mobile layaway savings platform that allows farmers to save and invest in farming inputs without taking credit.

leads.name: Agro Dynamics Limited
leads.profile: Agro Dynamics Limited is an agro-sourcing, processing and input supply company headquartered in Kampala, Uganda, with operations in Northern, Eastern, Central, and Western Uganda. Founded in 2009 by Henry Collins Kaliisa and Caroline Kamukama, the company initially focused on supplying white sorghum grain to Uganda Breweries Limited (a subsidiary of Diageo). The company has since expanded to include trade and supply of agro-inputs such as seeds, assorted fertilizers, agro and industrial chemicals, contract farming, produce aggregation and processing, manufacture of specialty fertilizers, and development of agro technologies. Key activities include: Contract farming with large-scale farmers (50+ acres) for grains like sorghum, maize, and sunflower; Aggregation, processing, sale and export of sorghum, maize, sunflower, soybean, malted barley, chia, and sesame grains to local and regional markets (Rwanda, Burundi); Manufacture of specialty fertilizers at a 140MT per day capacity factory in Egypt; Development of Agro Tracker, a proprietary digital solution for remote monitoring of farming activities, digital payments, procurement management, and weather forecasting. The company's vision is to leverage industry insights and expertise to provide innovative, high-quality, cost-effective technological solutions. Its mission is to promote sustainable agriculture practices, minimize environmental impact, and conserve resources. Core values include Resilience, Ethics, Quality, Unity, Innovation, Responsibility, and Excellence. The company has coverage of 0+ farmers, 0+ commercial agents, 0+ total acreage under cultivation, 0+ districts of operation, 0 MT+ upcountry storage capacity, 0+ years of experience, and 0 MT+ Kampala storage capacity. Products include grains (sorghum, soybeans, sunflower, maize, barley, sesame), fertilizers (Urea, DAP), agrochemicals (Zascomine 2,4 D-Amine Salt, Zascosate Glyphosate), peatmoss, and hybrid vegetable seeds (Tomato Whitney, Tomato Canelo F1 GSPP, Pepper Machado F1, Pepper Fenix F1, Onion Neptune F1). Partners include Koppert, Diamond, Equity Bank, dfcu Bank, Citibank, and others. The management team includes Henry Collins Kaliisa (Managing Director), Caroline Kamukama (Director), Gertjan De Groot (Partner), and Robbert Zwaan (Partner).

leads.name: Gloveg Agri Ltd
leads.profile:
# Gloveg Agri Ltd

Gloveg Agri Ltd is a Farming company. Founded in 2017. Headquartered in Kampala, Uganda.

## About
Gloveg Agri is an agribusiness and agricultural services company headquartered in Kampala, Uganda. The Company has a vision of accelerating food security across Africa and to become a major player in Africa's food production. Our services cut across the entire agricultural value chain that ranges from land acquisition, production, financial and insurance services, value addition, and marketing.
Why Choose Us
We deeply understand and appreciate that agriculture is the backbone and leading foreign exchange earner of our economy. Everyone in Uganda is desirous of participating in agricultural production to improve their income levels and food security. However, many people are fully and busily engaged in the corporate world with no or limited time to engage in agricultural production. Also, many people lack modern agricultural equipment and technical knowhow to engage in meaningful, productive and economically viable agricultural production. It is against this background that, Gloveg Agri Limited proposes to adopt a corporate farming model by deploying its modern machinery and manpower to cheaply undertake agricultural production on behalf of our busy corporate clients.

## Company Details
- Industry: Farming
- Type: Privately Held
- Headquarters: Kampala, Uganda
- Founded Year: 2017
- Homepage: glovegagric.com
- Aliases: gloveg agri ltd
- LinkedIn: linkedin.com/company/gloveg-agri-ltd
- Linkedin Followers: 93 (Yearly: +8.1%)

## Workforce
- Employees: 3
  - Yearly Growth: -25.0%
- Talent Sources: Africa Career Networks (1)
- Alumni Go To: Development Infrastructure (1)

leads.name: Grainpulse Limited
leads.profile: Grainpulse Limited is a leading agricultural sector company in Uganda, committed to providing food and nutrition security for every household in Africa. The company's vision is to be the leading provider of food and nutrition security in Africa, and its mission is to formalize and commercialize sustainable local agriculture by providing superior agronomic solutions and value chain expansion. Grainpulse operates across four main value chains: Coffee, Grain, Nutrition, and Agro-inputs. The company offers a range of products and services including Halisi Animal Feeds (compounded feeds and concentrates), agro-inputs and fertilizer blending (quality crop-specific NPK fertilizer blends), and agronomy and advisory services focused on providing best agronomic solutions to farmers. Grainpulse also has a strong focus on social impact and sustainability, aiming to increase yields through quality needs-based agro-inputs and agronomic advisory. The company is guided by core values of Integrity, Customer-Centricity, Performance, and Teamwork. Its offices are located at Plot 6/8 Nyondo Close, Bugolobi Industrial Area, P.O. Box 6217, Kampala, Uganda. Contact options include a toll-free line at 0800 300 099 and WhatsApp at +256 762 393350. The company also has a presence on Facebook, Instagram, and Twitter.

leads.name: SR Afro Chicks & Breeders Ltd
leads.profile: SR Afro Chicks & Breeders Ltd is a poultry company based in Uganda, in operation since 2014. Founded by a group of highly motivated individuals with years of experience in poultry rearing, the company is committed to delivering healthy, premium-quality day-old chicks. They rear chickens under hygienic conditions and sell dressed chicken as well as live birds under the brand name SR KUKU. Their products include day-old chicks, farm fresh brown eggs (SR KUKU eggs), commercial poultry feed, and dressed chicken. The company operates multiple farms: a 102.2-acre farm in Luweero, Uganda, with environment-controlled sheds for a bird population of 1,000,000 (commercial layer farming); 25-acre farms in Kijaguza and Kakoge housing broiler breeder facilities for 40,000 birds; and a 70-acre broiler farm (SR KUKU) across Uganda with an abattoir processing up to 16,000 birds per day. Eggs from the breeder farm are transported to a hatchery in Kampala. The company also has a presence in Kenya with an address in Nairobi. Contact details: Plot 147, next to Kida Guest House, Nsooba road, Off Mawanda Rd, Kampala, Uganda. Phone: +2562009966335, +256786087562. Kenya address: P.O.Box 37093-00200, Hari Industrial park, B4&B5, Plot No. 209/10638, Off.Mombasa Road, Behind Libra House, Masai Road, Nairobi, Kenya. Phone: +254781797777, +254739797777. Opening hours: Monday – Saturday 08:30 AM - 7:00 PM, Sunday closed. Outlets include Luweero, Ovino Complex - Kampala, Arua, Yumbe, Gulu, Lira, Mityana, Fortportal, Hoima, Mbarara, Kisoro, Iganga, Jinja, Mbale.

leads.name: Agro & More Agri-Business Developers Limited
leads.profile: Agro and More is an impact-driven youth-led social enterprise in Uganda. The company empowers smallholder farmers by transitioning them from subsistence to commercialized agriculture. They provide access to genuine and affordable farm inputs (seeds, fertilizers, insecticides, fungicides, irrigation pumps, farm tools), timely advisory services (private extension services, training for beginner farmers, farm workers, or farmer groups), and market support (local and export markets via Khamisa Foods Ltd). The company has over 0+ years of experience and is located at Mukasa Building, First Floor - Rm.05, Mpigi-Butambala Rd, Mpigi Town. Contact phone: +256 778 163 823. They have a team and offer consultancy services, with a blog and career opportunities. Client testimonials highlight successful partnerships with farmers like Grace Birungi (passion fruit farmer), Bogere Geofrey (ginger farmer), Beatrice (cabbage farmer), Mr. Okileje Benjamin (Chairperson, Kopedur Farmers Group), and Madam Abura Lydia (Chairperson, Akiyoson Ayesu Farmer’s Group), demonstrating improved yields, pest management, market access, and income.

leads.name: Napas Agro Processors Ltd
leads.profile: Napas Agro Processors is a licensed agribusiness company in Uganda, majoring in fruit farming and processing alongside animal production. The company started operations in 2021 and is fully registered under the Ugandan Companies Act. They produce Snap Juice, a premium fruit juice made from Ugandan-grown fruits, with a goal of enhancing prosperity through value addition and sustainable agriculture. The company aims to improve the value of Ugandan-farmed produce and develop products that compete internationally. They have over 4 products, 150+ distributors, and work with 500+ farmers. Their farm is located in Kitukutwe, Kira, Wakiso District, P.O. Box 108602, Kampala, Uganda. Contact phone: (+256) 394 509 864. The company is grounded in values of sustainability, innovation, and quality, and is recognized for its commitment to enhancing Ugandan agriculture while making a significant impact on the global stage. They offer opportunities to become a distributor or farmer partner.
